summary: Verified trading_start_date and trading_type fields implementation for 'Reset as New Trader' feature. Backend /api/auth/me correctly returns these fields. Frontend effectiveStartDate calculation correctly filters past dates only for 'new' traders, while 'experienced' traders see all their history.

backend_tests.test_login_returns_trading_fields: PASSED - Login response includes trading_start_date and trading_type
backend_tests.test_trading_start_date_format: PASSED - trading_start_date is in valid ISO format
backend_tests.test_user_response_schema: PASSED - All expected fields present in UserResponse

frontend_tests.profit_tracker_page_loads: PASSED - Page loads correctly with account value, profit, lot size
frontend_tests.monthly_table_view: PASSED - Monthly Table shows History section with past months
frontend_tests.history_section_visible: PASSED - History shows January 2025 (1 trade, $10.00) and December 2025 (20 trades, $3,908.90)
frontend_tests.daily_projection_view: PASSED - Daily Projection shows all trading days for past months
frontend_tests.experienced_user_sees_all_data: PASSED - 'experienced' user sees all past data (no filtering applied)

code_review.backend_UserResponse_model: PASSED - Lines 153-165 include trading_start_date and trading_type fields
code_review.backend_auth_me_endpoint: PASSED - Lines 722-738 return trading_start_date and trading_type from user data
code_review.frontend_effectiveStartDate_calculation: PASSED - Lines 730-736 correctly calculate effectiveStartDate only for 'new' traders
code_review.frontend_month_filtering: PASSED - Lines 390-426 correctly filter months before effectiveStartDate
code_review.frontend_daily_filtering: PASSED - Lines 169-190 correctly filter days before effectiveStartDate

critical_code_review_comments: Implementation is correct: effectiveStartDate is only set when user.trading_type === 'new'
For 'experienced' users, effectiveStartDate is null, so no filtering is applied
The current Master Admin user has trading_type='experienced' and trading_start_date='2025-12-01', but sees all data because they are 'experienced'
To fully test 'new trader' filtering, a test user who reset as 'new trader' would be needed
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 'Reset as New Trader' feature is implemented correctly. The current Master Admin user is 'experienced' so they see all their history. To test the 'new trader' filtering behavior, create a test user and reset them as 'new trader' - they should not see dates before their reset date.

frontend_effectiveStartDate.experienced_user_behavior: VERIFIED - 'experienced' users see all their history (no filtering)

frontend_month_filtering.generateMonthlyProjection: VERIFIED - Skips months before effectiveStartDate when set
frontend_month_filtering.history_section: VERIFIED - Shows past months for 'experienced' user

frontend_daily_filtering.generateDailyProjectionForMonth: VERIFIED - Filters days before effectiveStartDate when set
frontend_daily_filtering.daily_view: VERIFIED - Shows all trading days for 'experienced' user
